page: Amazon Web Services i8g.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture, this server features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate a highly capable memory subsystem, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top tier. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 462.12 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 96.68 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are strong, with gzip compression reaching 30.31 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.26 GB/sec. However, raw CPU performance is more moderate, with PassMark CPU scores falling into the weak category, and LLM inference speeds for text generation on a small 135M model registering at 59.57 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i8g.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring high-speed local storage and rapid memory access without the premium of high-core-count instances. The inclusion of dedicated physical cores on the Nitro hypervisor ensures consistent execution. This server is well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and data compression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of memory and a 937 GB local NVMe SSD, managed via the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results highlight a top-tier memory subsystem, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and cached read bandwidth reaching 462.12 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression performance is strong, though overall CPU-heavy compute and LLM inference benchmarks are relatively weak. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for storage-intensive and memory-sensitive workloads by pairing dedicated physical cores with high-speed local NVMe storage. It is highly suited for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 937 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance for databases.

tagline: An ARM-powered storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency I/O operations
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression capabilities
Optimized for database hosting, Redis caching, and static web serving
